a:r.a:t: Case Study: Building materials company
GTM strategy:
What changes to our product ladder and channel pricing will drive sustainable growth without eroding existing margins?
A multi-billion revenue building materials manufacturer with global operations
, mix of product and service lines with complicated B2D2B go-to-market model
Business performing well, but owners looking to unlock underserved market & differentiate offerings
Seeking to balance margin discipline with growth
and improve specific business unit with concerning outlook
Agreed to optimize sales force to “best practices”
via asset-enabled IDP findings and strategy engagement
Context
Impact: 4-5% ROS
In process (Impact initiatives launched, tracking results)
Example analysis/insights
Talent priorities:
Developed specific talent agenda for frontline sales and new role definition for sales managers
Customer channel alignment:
Set priorities for sales support and digital infrastructure to drive customer experience
Capability agenda:
Identified most beneficial sales capabilities given unsustainably strong performance
GTM model design:
Prepared for strategic expansion into new products and geographies
Performance goals:
Reinforced customer centricity to sustain market leading financial performance
McKinsey & Company
